metadata.name: Global Tariff Briefing
metadata.audience: NYT colleagues tracking tariffs and trade policy globally

all.headline: US Treasuries Fall as Inflation Angst Eclipses Haven Buying
all.description: US Treasuries fell as conflict in the Middle East sent oil prices soaring, stoking fear inflation will accelerate and forcing traders to scale back wagers on the scope of interest-rate cuts.

all.headline: Defense to Airlines: How Stocks Are Reacting to Iran Attacks
all.description: Shares of airlines, cruise operators and hoteliers fell on Monday as investors reacted to conflict in the Middle East. Energy and defense stocks jumped.

all.headline: US Says Three Fighter Jets Downed in 'Friendly Fire' Over Kuwait
Stocks Sell Off as US Attack on Iran Drives Oil Prices Higher
all.description: Gold rallies, oil prices surge as Strait of Hormuz all but closed to shipping.

all.headline: Emerging Assets Slump as Energy Costs Spike on Iran Conflict
all.description: Emerging-market currencies and stocks slumped as US and Israeli strikes on Iran are triggering a jump in energy prices and bring a rally in riskier assets to a screeching halt.

all.headline: Fuel Prices Jump More Than Oil as Iran War Hits Supply
all.description: Fuel prices surged across global markets on Monday as the war in Iran disrupted shipments from Persian Gulf refineries.

all.headline: Iran Conflict Disrupts Global Air Travel
all.description: Travel chaos extended through the Middle East and beyond, as carriers across the Persian Gulf extended blanket flight suspensions, causing major disruptions at some of the world’s busiest airports. (S

all.headline: Pakistan Stocks Fall Most on Record After Middle East War
all.description: A key stock gauge in Pakistan plunged the most on record after geopolitical tensions in the Middle East escalated following US and Israel’s strikes on Iran.

all.headline: Clean Max Shares Slip 18% in Worst Indian Debut Since 2021
all.description: Shares of Indian renewable energy firm Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions Ltd. tumbled 18% on their first day of trading, making it the worst debut in more than four years for an offering of a similar

all.headline: JPMorgan Tests ‘Art of the Possible’ in Leveraged Buyouts
all.description: When JPMorgan Chase & Co. took the lead last year in financing the $55 billion takeover of Electronic Arts Inc., a record-setting leveraged buyout, Wall Street saw it as a sign that a lucrative pe

all.headline: Oil prices jump and shares fall as conflict escalates
all.description: Experts have warned that a prolonged conflict could push global energy prices even higher.

all.headline: UK launches consultation asking for views on under-16s social media ban
all.description: Discussions over what measures to implement to protect children's wellbeing will last for three months.

all.headline: Brewdog closes all bars for a day as it looks to complete sale
all.description: The company brought in consultants AlixPartners last month, after failing to make a profit in recent years.

all.headline: Flights cancelled and new travel warnings issued after Iran strikes
all.description: BA and Virgin Atlantic are among major airlines to ground services to the Middle East in light of the attacks.

all.headline: Rent tops £1,000 a month in more areas - find out where
all.description: The cost of renting privately has surged in the last five years, but tenants may now see a slowdown.

all.headline: Deepfake attack: 'Many people could have been cheated'
all.description: The boss of the Bombay Stock Exchange was recently targeted in what is a growing global problem.

all.headline: A firm hiring blind staff went bust - but its mission lives on
all.description: The business had been running for over 150 years before it collapsed, so one former employee took matters into her own hands.

all.headline: Could a huge data centre revitalise Ayrshire - or ruin it?
all.description: Controversial proposals to turn land near HMP Kilmarnock into a technology hub are being advanced by energy company ILI Group.

all.headline: The third man who links Andrew with Jeffrey Epstein
all.description: For nearly a decade, David Stern acted as a key conduit between Jeffrey Epstein and Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.

all.headline: Trump orders government to stop using Anthropic in battle over AI use
all.description: The move announced on social media comes after a standoff between Anthropic's boss and the US Department of Defense.

all.headline: Allies of US in the Gulf bear brunt of Iran attacks
all.description: Iran's attacks on Gulf Arab states suggest the Islamic Republic is targeting not just the US military but also civilian infrastructure.

all.headline: Kidnapping of foreigners soars in Africa's lawless Sahel region
all.description: Growing insecurity in the Sahel made 2025 one of the worst years on record for the abduction of foreigners in Africa.

all.headline: Could Kim's teen daughter become North Korea's next leader?
all.description: Kim Jue Ae is becoming more visible alongside her father, but much about her remains a mystery.

all.headline: Michael B Jordan upends Oscars race as Sinners wins big at Actor Awards
all.description: The US star's best actor win for Sinners leaves the Oscars race too close to call.

all.headline: At least 153 dead after reported strike on school, Iran says
all.description: Iran has blamed the US and Israel for the strike; the US is looking into reports of the incident, while the IDF says it is "not aware" of operations in the area.

all.headline: Nine dead in missile attack on Israel as Iran strikes region
all.description: Several deaths are reported across the Middle East as Tehran retaliates for massive strikes by the US and Israel.

all.headline: Deadly Texas bar shooting 'potentially act of terrorism', FBI says
all.description: Two people were killed and 14 injured, with the suspected gunman, identified by some media outlets, also shot dead.

all.headline: War photographer Paul Conroy dies as tributes paid
all.description: Jamie Doran played the photographer, who has died aged 61, in the 2018 movie A Private War.

all.headline: Why is WhatsApp's privacy policy facing a legal challenge in India?
all.description: A 2021 policy update requires users to share data with Meta for ads purposes to continue using the app.

all.headline: Trump buries the 20th century
all.description: Donald Trump's legacy became clearer than ever with the attack on Iran's regime, the latest 20th century artifact to crumble in the Trump era.

all.headline: Israel strikes Lebanon, widening Mideast conflict
all.description: The attack on Hezbollah in Beirut marks a sharp escalation in the war that started with U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran.

all.headline: Meeting of EU ministers in Cyprus postponed after drone strike
all.description: European affairs ministers from around the bloc were due to arrive in Nicosia on Monday, but the spreading crisis in the Middle East has disrupted flights.

all.headline: Closing the nutritional gap in cancer care
all.description: As Europe redefines its health priorities, addressing persistent gaps like cancer-related malnutrition will be essential to ensure resilient systems and better outcomes for patients.

all.headline: British base in Cyprus hit by drone strike
all.description: The attack came shortly after U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he would allow the U.S. to use British military bases to “destroy [Iranian] missiles at source.”

all.description: Presented by Amazon

all.description: Presented by Amazon

all.description: Computer scientists are campaigning against the global march toward age checks online.
